content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(i) WARN — Privacy policy states "We do not collect, process, or store your location data," but the app uses CoreLocation with `startUpdatingLocation()` and stores a route array (App/RunSessionManager.swift:11-18), and the description advertises GPS run mapping (description.txt:1).
Pierre: Apple cares because a privacy policy that directly contradicts observable app behavior is a 5.1.1(i) rejection risk and misleads users about their data. The policy denies any location collection, yet the core feature literally records GPS coordinates into `route` — a flat contradiction, even if that data never leaves the device. Update the policy to accurately describe on-device location processing (and any iCloud sync) rather than denying it outright.
